name: Fossil Group, Inc.
business: Fossil Group designs, markets, and distributes consumer fashion accessories including watches, jewelry, handbags, small leather goods, belts, and sunglasses. The products are sold under owned brands (FOSSIL, SKAGEN, MICHELE, RELIC, ZODIAC) and licensed brands (ARMANI EXCHANGE, DIESEL, EMPORIO ARMANI, MICHAEL KORS, SKECHERS, TORY BURCH). Customers include wholesale accounts, direct-to-consumer via e-commerce and retail stores, and independent distributors across approximately 132 countries.

exposures.sentence: Watches are our core global business.

Our holistic e-commerce efforts include three forms of digital channels: first, our owned global e-commerce websites for our brands; second, we sell our products to leading third-party online retailers and our wholesalers e-commerce websites; third, we directly sell to consumers on major third-party platforms.
In addition, we face strong competition in the watch category from technology companies that offer alternatives to our traditional watches, such as Apple, Garmin, Google and Samsung.
Tariffs negatively impacted our gross margin by approximately 140 basis points during fiscal 2025.
Our supply chain may be disrupted by changes in U.S. trade policy with China or as a result of a pandemic.
The risks associated with climate change and other environmental impacts could negatively affect our business and operations.

In addition, as artificial intelligence ( AI ) technologies, including generative AI models, develop rapidly, threat actors are using these technologies to create new sophisticated attack methods that are increasingly automated, targeted and coordinated and more difficult to defend against.
